title: Can't `podman volume create` a tmpfs volume with size limit
body:
### Issue Description

I wanted to create a tmpfs volume with size limit to mount in a container, but it throws an error that it is unsupported, but I believe it should be, according to [discussion 20442](https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/20442). Initially hit on 4.7.0 and also tried on 4.7.1 with the same results.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```bash
→ podman volume create --opt type=tmpfs --opt device=tmpfs --opt o=size=1M mytmp
Error: volume options size and inodes not supported. Filesystem does not support Project Quota
```

### Describe the results you received

Error: volume options size and inodes not supported. Filesystem does not support Project Quota

### Describe the results you expected

I expected the volume creation to succeed because tmpfs supports size limit when used in a `run` command like:

```bash
→ podman run --rm --mount=type=tmpfs,tmpfs-size=1M,destination=/out fedora bash -c 'yes > /out/foo'
yes: standard output: No space left on device
```

### podman info output
